changes_summary: The critique does not show that the plan was aimed at the wrong root cause. A single shared mobile/touch interaction policy is still the right approach because the repo already has one shell, one timeline stack, and one persistence gate. The revision narrows the fix to the actual missing layers: it now explicitly routes the policy through `VideoEditorProvider` and the split `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` contexts, and it separates baseline device class from per-event input modality so iPad touch behavior cannot accidentally override mouse/trackpad behavior on the same device.

success_criteria.criterion: The plan identifies concrete mobile/iPad touch points in the existing editor stack, including provider/context wiring, shell layout, timeline canvas, clip drag, trim, playhead scrub, inspector, overlay editing, and persistence/sync gating.
The plan defines device-specific interaction categories and gesture mapping for phone touch, iPad touch, and iPad mouse/trackpad plus keyboard behavior.
The plan defines a single interaction policy/state model that extends the current context and `interactionStateRef` patterns instead of introducing a parallel mobile-only state or persistence path.

The plan explicitly threads the shared interaction policy through `VideoEditorProvider` and the split `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` contexts so the named consumers can access the same source of truth.

The plan resolves gesture behavior from both device class and per-event input modality such as `pointerType`, so iPad mouse/trackpad input preserves desktop-style behavior while iPad touch gets touch-specific rules.

The plan specifies scroll-vs-edit conflict rules, selection/move/trim/context behavior, inspector strategy, precision editing behavior, and accessibility requirements in an implementation-actionable way.
The plan includes phased implementation steps with concrete file-level insertion points and explicit execution and validation order.
The proposed implementation reuses the current editor shell, timeline, provider, and persistence structures instead of creating a separate mobile timeline component.
Validation starts with focused policy and regression tests before broader manual device checks.
Manual smoke coverage includes iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad.

assumptions: Use the repo's existing phone/tablet buckets from `src/shared/hooks/mobile/index.ts` as the baseline device-class signal; this plan does not introduce new responsive breakpoints.

Modern Safari and other target browsers expose `PointerEvent.pointerType` reliably enough to distinguish touch from mouse/trackpad on iPad, so gesture policy can be resolved per event instead of per device alone.

The split editor contexts published by `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx` remain the consumer API for shell, timeline, preview, and inspector surfaces; phase 1 should thread the new interaction policy through those contexts rather than bypass them.
Desktop and hardware-keyboard workflows should remain intact; touch-specific behavior only changes gesture handling when the active modality is touch or when an explicit mobile editing mode is armed.
Phone phase 1 should stay inspector-first for overlay transforms and track reassignment, with direct on-canvas manipulation prioritized on iPad first.
`interactionStateRef` must remain the only persistence/poll deferral mechanism for touch and mouse gestures alike.
